mission.title: Install and Configure Minikube
mission.description: Minikube is a tool that allows you to run Kubernetes locally on your machine. It's ideal for developers who want to test and develop applications in a Kubernetes environment without needing a full cloud setup.

steps.title: Install Minikube
steps.description:
Download and install Minikube v1.34. Use the following command to install it on Linux:
```bash
curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/minikube/releases/v1.34.0/minikube-linux-amd64
sudo install minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube
```
Verify the installation:
```bash
minikube version
```

steps.title: Start Minikube Cluster
steps.description:
Start a Minikube cluster with Kubernetes version 1.30:
```bash
minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30.0
```
This command initializes a local Kubernetes cluster with the specified version.

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
Set up kubectl to connect to your Minikube cluster:
```bash
kubectl config use-context minikube
```
Verify the cluster is running:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get nodes
```

steps.title: Enable Dashboard
steps.description:
Enable the Kubernetes dashboard for easier management:
```bash
minikube dashboard
```
This command opens the dashboard in your default web browser.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Enable the metrics-server addon for monitoring:
```bash
minikube addons enable metrics-server
```
Verify that the metrics-server is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Stop and Delete Minikube Cluster
uninstall.description:
To stop and delete the Minikube cluster, run:
```bash
minikube stop
minikube delete
```
This will remove the local Kubernetes cluster and free up resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Minikube
upgrade.description:
To upgrade Minikube to the latest version (v1.34), first back up your configurations and data:
```bash
minikube config view > minikube-config-backup.yaml
```
Then, upgrade Minikube:
```bash
minikube update-check
minikube stop
minikube delete
curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/minikube/releases/v1.34.0/minikube-linux-amd64
sudo install minikube-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/minikube
minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30.0
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
minikube version
kubectl version
```

troubleshooting.title: Minikube fails to start
troubleshooting.description:
If Minikube fails to start, check the logs:
```bash
minikube logs
```
Common issues include insufficient memory or CPU allocation. Ensure your system meets the requirements and adjust the resources with:
```bash
minikube start --memory=4096 --cpus=2
```

troubleshooting.title: kubectl not connecting to Minikube
troubleshooting.description:
If kubectl cannot connect, ensure you are using the correct context:
```bash
kubectl config current-context
```
If it’s not set to 'minikube', switch contexts:
```bash
kubectl config use-context minikube
```

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If the dashboard does not open, check if it is enabled:
```bash
minikube addons list
```
If it is not enabled, run:
```bash
minikube addons enable dashboard
```

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance and stability, added support for new Kubernetes features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain flags related to VM drivers.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new addons and improved compatibility with Kubernetes 1.30.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for older Kubernetes versions.

resolution.summary: A successful setup includes a running Minikube cluster with kubectl configured to connect to it, the Kubernetes dashboard accessible, and the metrics-server enabled for monitoring.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl and minikube installed on your local machine.
